buildSteps.label: A sudden change in brightness
buildSteps.description: When neighboring pixels jump from dark to light (or light to dark), our eyes see a boundary

buildSteps.label: Blur REMOVES edges
buildSteps.description: Averaging smooths out sharp transitions, which is why blurred images look soft and boundary-free

buildSteps.label: So edge detection is the OPPOSITE of blur
buildSteps.description: Instead of averaging neighbors to smooth things out, we look at the difference between neighbors to find where things change

buildSteps.label: The key question
buildSteps.description: How do we measure "change" between neighboring pixels? Sounds like a job for... a kernel!
